headline: Sarawak Launches State-Owned Airline SkyBorneo
context: Sarawak government-backed SkyBorneo commenced operations in February 2026 with 6 ATR-72 aircraft serving underserved rural routes.

cards.big: SkyBorneo launched 6 rural routes with ATR-72 aircraft February 2026
cards.sub: State-backed carrier aims to connect 12 remote airstrips abandoned by MASwings

cards.big: MASwings discontinued 8 rural Sarawak routes in 2024 citing RM45 million losses
cards.sub: Communities in Bario Long Lellang and Ba Kelalan lost their only air connections

cards.big: SkyBorneo received RM280 million state subsidy for first 3 years operations
cards.sub: Fares capped at RM150 one-way compared to MASwings previous RM280 average

cards.big: 6 aircraft serve Kuching-Bario Miri-Long Lellang and 4 other highland routes
cards.sub: Fleet expansion to 12 aircraft planned by 2028 to add Sabah cross-border services

cards.big: State airlines test whether Sarawak can build connectivity that federal operators abandoned
SkyBorneo is Sarawak's answer to airfares that cost 3 times the peninsular rate per kilometre.
